paragraphs.en: He called me. I want to be clear about that. I didn’t call Him. He invited me up personally. Very selective crowd. Very hard ticket. I’ve met with many world leaders—Putin, Kim Jong Un, Jared—and I would say God’s in that tier. In terms of money, probably higher than most of them. We had a great table. God has tremendous clout.
paragraphs.zh: 是他打来的，我要说清楚。我没有打给他，他是亲自邀请我的。人都非常有选择性，门槛也非常高。我见过很多世界领导人——普京、金正恩、贾里德——而且我要说上帝在那个级别。按金钱算，可能还高于他们大多数人。我们坐了张非常好的桌子。上帝的人脉和影响力极其惊人。

paragraphs.en: Incredible gates. Strong. A lot of harp people hovering. Maybe too many, if we’re being honest. And the little fat bellhops—cherubs, they call them—wings too small for the body. Slow with luggage. I didn’t tip. The show! Better than Vegas. Volcanoes lighting up the sky, or whatever you call it. World-class earthquakes. I said, “God, I’m ashamed to admit it, but the earthquakes we have on Earth don’t compare.”
paragraphs.zh: 门口的门太壮观了。气势强大。很多拿竖琴的人在空中盘旋。老实说，或许有点太多了。还有那些小胖胖的铃铛侍者——他们叫小天使——翅膀对身体来说太小了，拿行李动作很慢。我没有给小费。那场景表演太夸张了，胜过拉斯维加斯。像火山点亮天空一样，叫什么都行。世界级的地震。我说：“上帝，我得老实说，地球上的地震完全比不上这个。”

paragraphs.en: He was very complimentary. He said, “They call them Acts of God, but, after what you’ve accomplished, maybe we should start calling them Acts of President.” Which was nice. Somebody asked me before I came out here, “Was He taller than you expected?” I said, “About what I expected.”
paragraphs.zh: 他非常恭维我。他说：“大家把它叫做‘天赐行为’，但看你做成了这么多事，也许我们该改叫‘总统行为’。”这还挺好的。有人在我出来之前问我：“他比你想象得更高吗？”我说：“大概和我预想差不多。”

paragraphs.en: Then God let me smite a guy trying to parallel park. Which was a very small part of the meeting, by the way. People are asking why we didn’t discuss the suffering of innocent children. It came up. I know children. His son was bullied. Good for ratings, by the way. Then we discussed important issues. Very positive discussions about clouds. I want clouds over Washington, but not the poor neighborhoods. Strong clouds. Not wispy. Some of His clouds—and I don’t want this to get beyond this room and Truth Social—were too fluffy. “Another thing, God,” I said. “You got tremendous demand up here, but everybody’s dead. Why not go members only?”
paragraphs.zh: 然后上帝让我砍了一个在找平行停车位的人。顺便说一句，这是整场会见里很小的一部分。人们在问我们为什么没讨论无辜孩子的受苦。这个话题有提到。我知道那些孩子。他的儿子被欺负过。顺带一提，这对收视率很有帮助。然后我们讨论了重要议题。关于云层的讨论很积极。我想让华盛顿上空有云，但别让贫困社区被笼罩。云要厚实的，不要那种轻飘飘的。有些他的云——我不希望这件事传出这个房间和 Truth Social——太蓬松了。“还有一点，上帝，”我说，“你在这里人气很高，可是人都没了，为什么不改成只对会员开放？”

paragraphs.en: “Frankly, God,” I said, “can I be frank?” I didn’t say “regime change,” but He knew what I meant. He asked me how the Miami Marlins were doing. I think He wanted tickets. “Look,” I said, “I came here for a reason. Nobody’s saying you did a bad job, but...” People tell me, “Mr. President, you put the fear of God in God.” He knew I could back this up militarily. Operation I Mean It. Or, Operation I’m Not Going to Tell You Again. Or, Operation I’ll Give You Something to Cry About!
paragraphs.zh: “说实话吧，神，”我开口说，“我能不能也直说？”我没有说“政权更迭”这三个字，但他知道我的意思。他问我迈阿密马林鱼队最近怎么样。我想他可能想要门票。“听着，”我说，“我来这儿是有原因的。没人说你做得不好，但……”人们对我说：“总统先生，你让上帝都感到了敬畏。”他明白我可以用军事手段来支撑这点。所谓“我是真心的行动”。或者，“我再说一遍我可不想再重复”的行动。或者，“我会给你点值得哭一场的东西”的行动！

paragraphs.en: While God did God things, I looked around for the gift shop. You know the thing about that place? Bad signage. I know more than He does about real estate. Don’t know who His lighting guy is, but the sun shouldn’t come down so fast. You’re outside eating dinner, and the next thing you know it’s dark.
paragraphs.zh: 在上帝办他那些神圣事的时候，我四处找礼品店。你知道那个地方有个问题吗？导视做得很差。我比他更懂房地产。那灯光师是谁我就不知道了，但太阳不应该这么快就落下。你在外面吃饭，下一秒就暗下来。

paragraphs.en: Did I get everything? Look, when I walked in, Earth was one way. When I left? Very different conversation. Many, many historians are saying it was the most productive meeting since Zeus met with President McKinley. I can’t share everything, but I will say things are on the table that weren’t even on the floor. I can say we’re very close to getting my parking ticket validated.
paragraphs.zh: 我都拿到了吗？你看，我进来时地球是这样；我离开时？那是完全不同的对话。很多很多历史学家都在说，这次会见是自宙斯与麦金利总统会晤以来最有成效的一次。我不能把所有细节都说出来，但我可以说，桌上有些东西，甚至连地上都还没出现。我可以说，我们离把我的停车罚单核销成功，已经很接近了。

paragraphs.en: His last words to me were “You know, of everyone I’ve ever created, you might be the one I’m proudest of.” I probably shouldn’t say this, but when I left He cried a little.
paragraphs.zh: 他对我说的最后一句话是：“你知道吗，在我所创造过的所有人里，你可能是我最值得自豪的那一个。”我可能不该这么说，但当我离开时，他有点哭了。

paragraphs.en: Update: Unnamed sources say that the Commander-in-Chief is evaluating a potential 2028 bid for God. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 更新：不具名消息人士称，司令官在评估一项对上帝可能的2028年竞选。♦
